title: fix(infra): repair mealie dns resolution in prod
description:
# fix(infra): repair mealie dns resolution in prod

## Context
Mealie application is currently inaccessible in the Production environment.

## Problem
- `curl -I https://mealie.truxonline.com` fails with connection timeout.
- DNS resolution for `mealie.truxonline.com` returns a public IP (`217.70.184.38`) instead of the expected Internal VIP (`192.168.201.70`).
- ExternalDNS logs show `Skipping endpoint ... because owner id does not match, found: "", required: "prod-unifi"`.

## Root Cause
ExternalDNS cannot take ownership of the existing DNS record (likely created manually or by another controller without the correct owner ID) because of the TXT registry protection mechanism.

## Goal
Force ExternalDNS to take ownership of the record or manually correct the record so Mealie becomes accessible internally.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `dig +short mealie.truxonline.com` returns `192.168.201.70` (from internal network).
- [ ] `curl -I https://mealie.truxonline.com` returns HTTP 200/30x.
notes: PHASE:0 - Tâche démarrée (branch: dev)
close_reason: Fixed Mealie DNS resolution in prod by removing explicit target annotation in Ingress. Validated with curl (HTTP 200).

title: chore(maintenance): add cleanup script for empty ReplicaSets
description:
## Context
Over time, Kubernetes accumulates empty ReplicaSets from deployments updates. While not critical, they clutter `kubectl get rs` output.

## Current State
- Dev: 0 empty RS ✅
- Prod: 0 empty RS ✅
- Clean now, but will accumulate over time

## Task
Create automated cleanup script for empty ReplicaSets.

## Implementation
```bash
# scripts/cleanup-empty-replicasets.sh
#\!/bin/bash
# Delete ReplicaSets with 0 desired, 0 current, 0 ready

KUBECONFIG=${1:-./kubeconfig}
DRY_RUN=${2:-false}

kubectl --kubeconfig=$KUBECONFIG get rs -A -o json |   jq -r '.items[] | select(.spec.replicas == 0 and .status.replicas == 0) | 
    "kubectl delete rs -n \(.metadata.namespace) \(.metadata.name)"' |   if [ "$DRY_RUN" = "false" ]; then bash; else cat; fi
```

## Usage
```bash
# Dry run
just cleanup-rs dev --dry-run

# Execute
just cleanup-rs dev

# Add to justfile
cleanup-rs env='dev' dry='false':
  scripts/cleanup-empty-replicasets.sh terraform/environments/{{env}}/kubeconfig-{{env}} {{dry}}
```

## Safety
- Only deletes RS with 0/0/0 (desired/current/ready)
- Dry run by default
- Per-environment execution

## Scheduling
Optional: Add to monthly maintenance task or CI/CD

title: fix(backup): correct recursive exclusion patterns in rclone for litestream dirs
description:
## Root Cause
Pattern `--exclude ".*-litestream"` only matches at root level, not in subdirectories.

## Affected Apps
- sabnzbd (DB in /config/admin/ subdirectory)
- Potentially others with nested DB paths

## Fix Required
Replace in ALL apps with litestream+rclone:
```bash
# OLD (buggy)
--exclude ".*-litestream"
--exclude ".*-litestream/**"

# NEW (recursive)
--exclude "**/*-litestream"
--exclude "**/*-litestream/**"
--exclude "**/*.db-wal"
--exclude "**/*.db-shm"
```

## Files to Update
- apps/20-media/*/base/deployment.yaml (all *arr apps)
- apps/10-home/*/base/deployment.yaml
- apps/60-services/*/base/deployment.yaml
- apps/template-app/base/deployment.yaml

## Validation
1. kustomize build each app
2. Restart affected pods
3. Verify no .ltx corruption in litestream logs
close_reason: Fixed with PR #2090 - recursive exclusions + probe fix

title: derive: m11 - Annotations VPA discordantes
description:
**DÉRIVE MINEURE m11**

Description:
Annotations VPA utilisent plusieurs variantes:
- goldilocks.fairwinds.com/enabled
- vpa.kubernetes.io/updateMode
- etc.

Solution: Unifier vers vpa.kubernetes.io/updateMode (standard K8s) (30min)

État: non statué | Risque: LOW | Effort: 30min
close_reason: False positive: goldilocks.fairwinds.com/enabled (on Namespace) and vpa.kubernetes.io/updateMode (on Deployment) serve different purposes. Goldilocks annotation enables VPA creation for a namespace, while updateMode controls VPA behavior on workloads. Both are needed and not discordant.

title: eval(storage): evaluate Synology CSI v1.2.1 (critical bugfixes)
description:
## Contexte

Evaluate et upgrader Synology CSI de v1.2.0 → v1.2.1 (26 commits de bugfixes depuis v1.2.0).

### v1.2.1 upstream (tag non publié, disponible sur main = image 'edge')
- **fix #29**: Allow enabling space reclamation and FUA/Sync Cache SCSI commands for LUNs (critical iSCSI I/O fix)
- Add pod-security labels to namespace
- commit HEAD: 6547268b

### État actuel (2026-03-17)

**Dev cluster (phase 1 - ACTIVE) :**
- node DaemonSet : ✅ Running sur 'edge' (v1.2.1) depuis ~30min
- Logs confirmés : 'version=1.2.1', GRPC healthy, connexion DSM OK
- Zéro restart, zéro erreur
- controller StatefulSet : déjà sur 'edge' en base (pré-existant)
- PR #2149 mergée, prod-stable tag mis à jour

**Prod cluster :**
- node DaemonSet : toujours sur v1.2.0 (inchangé - intentionnel)
- controller StatefulSet : sur 'edge' (pré-existant en base, to fix)
- Note : inconsistance controller=edge / node=v1.2.0 pré-existante à corriger lors de la promotion

### Observation en cours

Période de surveillance dev : 2026-03-17 → 2026-03-24 (1 semaine)

Critères de validation :
- [ ] Aucun mount failure PVC
- [ ] Aucune déconnexion iSCSI
- [ ] Aucune I/O error dans les logs
- [ ] Aucun restart du pod node
- [ ] Volumes provisionnés correctement (test create/delete PVC)

## Travail restant (Phase 2)

Une fois la semaine de validation passée :

1. **Pinning version** : remplacer 'edge' par 'v1.2.1' dans base/node.yaml et base/controller.yaml
   - Actuellement node.yaml = v1.2.0 → v1.2.1
   - Actuellement controller.yaml = edge → v1.2.1
2. **PR** : upd base pour prod avec tag fixe
3. **Promotion prod** : merge + tag prod-stable

## Commandes de monitoring

```bash
export KUBECONFIG=.secrets/dev/kubeconfig-dev
# Health check
kubectl -n synology-csi get pods
# Logs erreurs
kubectl -n synology-csi logs -l app=synology-csi-node -c synology-csi-plugin --since=24h | grep -i 'error\|warn\|fail'

```

title: refactor(gitops): migrate to trunk-based development workflow
description:
Migrate from Git-Flow (dev branch) to Trunk-Based Development (main branch only).

Goal: Simplify the deployment workflow by removing the persistent 'dev' branch.

How:
1. Update all ArgoCD Applications in 'argocd/overlays/dev' to point to 'targetRevision: main' instead of 'dev'.
2. Verify that 'overlays/dev' configurations are correctly present in 'main'.
3. Delete the 'dev' branch from the repository.
4. Update CI/CD workflows and documentation (WORKFLOW.md) to reflect the new process (PR -> Main -> Prod Tag).

Result:
- DEV environment syncs automatically from 'main' (HEAD).
- PROD environment syncs from 'prod-stable' tag (promoted from main).
- No more complex merges between dev and main.

title: fix(kyverno): false positives on require-resource-requests - add preconditions for sizing labels
description:
# Problème: False Positives Kyverno sur require-resource-requests

## Contexte

Le maturity controller montre que la plupart des apps sont à `tier=none` avec `missing=bronze`, alors qu'elles avaient Silver/Gold avant.

## Cause

Les politiques de validation `require-resource-requests` et `check-image-tag` utilisent `background: true`.

**Problème**: Le background scan vérifie le manifeste **original** (sans resources), pas le résultat **muté** par `sizing-v2-mutate`.

### Preuve:
- Le pod n8n a les resources après mutation: `cpu: 50m, memory: 512Mi`
- Mais le PolicyReport dit: `fail` avec `source: background scan`
- Timestamp: 2026-03-15 11:00:52

### Commit problématique:
**#2079** a split `require-resources` en Bronze/Silver mais les nouvelles politiques n'ont PAS de precondition pour exclure les pods avec `vixens.io/sizing.*` labels.

## Solution suggérée

### Option 1 (Recommandée): Ajouter des preconditions

Ajouter dans `require-resource-requests.yaml` et `check-image-tag.yaml`:

```yaml
preconditions:
  any:
    - key: "{{ request.object.metadata.labels | keys(@) | [?starts_with(@, 'vixens.io/sizing.')] | length(@) }}"
      operator: Equals
      value: "0"
```

Cela exclura les pods qui ont déjà un sizing label (la mutation Kyverno ajoutera les resources).

### Option 2: Utiliser mutateExistingOnPolicyUpdate

Activer `mutateExistingOnPolicyUpdate: true` dans le sizing policy pour forcer la mutation sur les resources existantes.

### Fichiers à modifier:
- `apps/00-infra/kyverno/base/policies/require-resource-requests.yaml`
- `apps/00-infra/kyverno/base/policies/require-resource-limits.yaml`  
- `apps/00-infra/kyverno/base/policies/check-image-tag.yaml`

## Référence

- Doc Kyverno: Background scanning "has no effect on mutate rules for reporting"
- Le pod est créé APRÈS le sizing policy (2026-03-15 vs 2026-03-09)
close_reason:
Implemented coherent sizing validation system. PR #2151.

CHANGES:
1. require-resource-requests.yaml (Bronze): foreach-per-container, skip sizing-labeled containers → no more false positives
2. require-resource-limits.yaml (Silver): same approach
3. check-explicit-resources.yaml: new warning for explicit resources anti-pattern
4. sizing-v2-mutate.yaml: V-* cpuLim 10x → 4x (consistent with memLim 4x)
5. sizing-vpa-generate.yaml: auto-detect trigger (no more sizing-v2 dependency), RequestsAndLimits, maxAllowed defaults
6. Removed vixens.io/sizing-v2 from 53 app files

Expected: maturity controller shows real violations only (28 Bronze apps without sizing labels)

title: feat(just): add hibernate/unhibernate commands for app management
description:
## Feature Request
Add `just hibernate` and `just unhibernate` commands to easily manage app replicas.

## Usage
```bash
just hibernate homeassistant    # Set replicas=0
just unhibernate homeassistant  # Set replicas=1
just hibernated                 # List hibernated apps
```

## Implementation
1. **justfile commands:**
   - `hibernate <app>`: Patch kustomization.yaml to add replicas=0
   - `unhibernate <app>`: Remove replicas patch or set to 1
   - `hibernated`: List apps with replicas=0

2. **Script logic:**
   - Find app in apps/ directory
   - Edit overlay/dev/kustomization.yaml
   - Add/remove replicas patch
   - Commit + push (GitOps)
   - Wait for ArgoCD sync

3. **Safety checks:**
   - Verify app exists
   - Confirm before hibernating prod apps
   - Show current replica count

## Example Output
```bash
$ just hibernate homeassistant
📦 App: homeassistant (10-home)
🔍 Current replicas: 1
⚠️  Setting replicas to 0 (hibernated)
✅ Patch applied to apps/10-home/homeassistant/overlays/dev/kustomization.yaml
🔄 Commit: chore(homeassistant): hibernate app (replicas=0)
⏳ Waiting for ArgoCD sync...
✅ homeassistant hibernated successfully
```

## Dependencies
- vixens-f8ch (hibernation doc update)

## Related
- docs/procedures/dev-hibernation.md
- ADR-014 (dev hibernation strategy)

title: bug(dev): fix argocd-repo-server dns timeout
description:
Le contrôleur ArgoCD n'arrive pas à générer les manifestes car il échoue à résoudre `argocd-repo-server`.

**Symptômes :**
- Logs argocd-application-controller : `rpc error: code = Unavailable desc = connection error: desc = "transport: Error while dialing: dial tcp: lookup argocd-repo-server on 10.96.0.10:53: read udp 10.244.2.133:43630->10.96.0.10:53: i/o timeout"`
- Applications bloquées en état `Unknown` ou `OutOfSync` (vixens-app-of-apps).

**Pistes :**
- Vérifier CoreDNS (logs, restarts).
- Vérifier connectivité réseau (Cilium) entre le nœud du contrôleur et CoreDNS.
- Redémarrer `argocd-repo-server` et `argocd-application-controller`.
notes:
PHASE:3
INVESTIGATION COMPLETED:

ROOT CAUSE FOUND:
- Cilium DNS proxy transparent mode enabled
- Proxy at 169.254.116.108 cannot reach upstream DNS (192.168.208.70)
- CoreDNS logs: timeout errors on DNS forwards
- 1 CoreDNS pod: 68 restarts since 2026-01-06

APPS UNKNOWN STATUS:
- NOT caused by DNS timeout
- Caused by config errors (broken paths, typos)
- 5 apps affected: cloudnative-pg-crds, argocd-image-updater, media-namespace, external-dns-unifi-secrets, vixens-app-of-apps

CURRENT STATE:
- DNS stable for now (after CoreDNS restarts)
- No active DNS errors in ArgoCD logs
- Problem will recur without permanent fix

FOLLOW-UP TASKS CREATED:
- vixens-n8tr: Fix/delete 5 Unknown apps (P2)
- vixens-l31o: Disable Cilium DNS proxy (P1 - permanent fix)

NO CODE CHANGES NEEDED in ArgoCD scope.
close_reason: Investigation completed. Root cause identified: Cilium DNS proxy transparent mode causing CoreDNS timeouts. DNS currently stable after CoreDNS restarts. Apps Unknown status unrelated to DNS (config errors). Follow-up tasks created: vixens-l31o (P1 Cilium fix), vixens-n8tr (P2 cleanup Unknown apps). No code changes required in ArgoCD scope.

title: feat(media): migrate Jellyseerr to Seerr (merged project)
description:
## Context

Jellyseerr and Overseerr have merged into **Seerr** (Feb 2026). Jellyseerr is no longer maintained and should be migrated.

## Why This is Critical

- **Maintenance**: Jellyseerr development stopped (merged into Seerr)
- **Features**: Seerr combines best of both projects
- **Security**: Active development = security patches

## Technical Details

**Current State:**
- Image: `fallenbagel/jellyseerr:2.7`
- Database: SQLite in PVC
- Integrations: Jellyfin, Sonarr, Radarr, Prowlarr

**Target State:**
- Image: `ghcr.io/seerr-team/seerr:latest`
- Database: Compatible (SQLite schema preserved)
- Integrations: Preserved

## Migration Steps

1. **Backup Current State:**
   - Export Jellyseerr database
   - Document current config (Jellyfin URL, API keys)
   - Backup PVC: `apps/20-media/jellyseerr/overlays/prod/pvc.yaml`

2. **Update Manifest:**
   - Change image to `ghcr.io/seerr-team/seerr:latest`
   - Keep existing PVC (database compatible)
   - Update docs: `docs/applications/20-media/jellyseerr.md`

3. **Deploy and Test:**
   - Apply via ArgoCD
   - Verify database migration (auto-migration on first start)
   - Test integrations: Jellyfin, *arr stack
   - Validate user authentication

4. **Rename (Optional):**
   - Rename app jellyseerr → seerr (if desired)
   - Update ingress URLs
   - Update documentation references

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Current config backed up
- [ ] Image updated to Seerr
- [ ] App deployed successfully
- [ ] Database migrated automatically
- [ ] Jellyfin integration working
- [ ] Sonarr/Radarr/Prowlarr integrations working
- [ ] User authentication preserved
- [ ] Documentation updated

## Resources

- Seerr GitHub: https://github.com/seerr-team/seerr
- Migration notes: Check release notes for breaking changes
- Jellyseerr backup: Use `kubectl cp` for database

## Risks

- Database migration issues (mitigate: backup before)
- API key changes (verify after migration)
- User session loss (users re-login)

## Time Estimate

- Backup: 30 min
- Migration: 1 hour
- Testing: 1 hour
- **Total: ~2.5 hours**
close_reason: Migrated to ghcr.io/seerr-team/seerr:v3.1.0 in PR #2165. Drop-in: same PVC, port, env vars. Auto-migrates SQLite on first boot.

title: tech-debt(synology-csi): decide maturity exemption or silverification
description:
## Context

Synology CSI (synology-csi-node + synology-csi-controller) currently Bronze tier.

## Critical Infrastructure Assessment

**Criticality:** EXTREME
- 41 PVCs depend on this CSI driver
- Single point of failure for ALL persistent storage
- DaemonSet runs privileged with hostNetwork/hostPID
- Storage disruption = cluster-wide data loss risk

## Current Issues

1. Controller uses 'edge' tag (unstable, auto-updates)
2. Version mismatch: Controller (edge) vs Node (v1.2.0)
3. Manual resources present (anti-pattern, but vendor-recommended)
4. No sizing labels (not following cluster standards)

## Decision Required

**Option A: EXEMPTION (Recommended by analysis)**
- Pros: Minimal risk, vendor recommendations preserved
- Cons: CSI drivers remain outside maturity system
- Action: Create 'Critical Infrastructure' tier, document in ADR

**Option B: SILVERIFICATION**
- Pros: Follows cluster standards consistently
- Cons: HIGH RISK - storage failure affects 50+ apps
- Requirements: Conservative sizing, extensive testing, rollback plan

**Option C: PARTIAL (Pin version only)**
- Pin controller to v1.2.0 (eliminate 'edge' tag)
- Leave resources as-is (vendor defaults)
- Defer maturity system participation

## Analysis Available

See session summary 2026-03-13 for full risk analysis:
- Deployment structure (Kustomize, vendor manifests)
- Dependent resources (41 PVCs across 50+ apps)
- Modification risks (catastrophic failure scenarios)

## Next Steps

1. Review risk analysis from explore agents
2. Decide on exemption vs silverification
3. If exempt: Create ADR, update maturity controller
4. If silverify: Create detailed plan with testing strategy
5. If partial: Pin version, defer maturity decision

## Related

- Part of Silverification Campaign cleanup
- Remaining Bronze apps: 7 total (2 CSI + 3 ArgoCD + velero + goldilocks)
- Campaign target: All non-infrastructure apps ≥ Silver

description:
## Context

The docs/ directory has evolved with multiple documentation layers. After recent consolidation of reports/, need to audit the entire docs/ structure for consistency and gaps.

## Objectives

### 1. Inventory & Audit
- Review all documentation in docs/:
  - docs/README.md (main hub)
  - docs/guides/
  - docs/reference/
  - docs/procedures/
  - docs/applications/
  - docs/adr/
  - docs/reports/ (recently consolidated ✅)
  - docs/troubleshooting/
  - Root-level docs (CLAUDE.md, WORKFLOW.md, etc.)

- Identify:
  - Duplicate information
  - Outdated content
  - Missing documentation
  - Broken internal links
  - Inconsistent formatting

### 2. Consolidation Opportunities
- Check for overlapping content between:
  - docs/guides/ vs docs/procedures/
  - docs/reference/ vs docs/applications/
  - Root-level READMEs vs docs/README.md
- Merge or cross-reference as appropriate
- Follow DRY principle (single source of truth)

### 3. Documentation Gaps
- Verify all applications in apps/ have docs in docs/applications/
- Check that all ADRs are referenced in docs/adr/README.md
- Ensure all guides have corresponding reference documentation
- Validate troubleshooting guides cover common issues

### 4. Link Validation
- Check all internal links (markdown references)
- Verify cross-references between documents
- Update any broken links from recent restructuring
- Consider adding link checker to CI/CD

### 5. Consistency
- Standardize markdown formatting:
  - Heading hierarchy (H1 for titles, H2 for sections)
  - Code block language tags
  - Table formatting
  - List styles (- vs *)
- Consistent frontmatter where applicable
- Uniform "Last Updated" dates format

### 6. Navigation
- Update docs/README.md if structure changes
- Ensure clear navigation paths
- Add breadcrumbs where helpful
- Consider table of contents for long documents

## Deliverables

- [ ] Documentation audit report (list of issues found)
- [ ] Consolidated/merged documents (track what was removed)
- [ ] Updated docs/README.md with accurate structure
- [ ] Fixed broken links
- [ ] Identified missing documentation (create follow-up tasks)
- [ ] Updated "Last Updated" dates on modified files

## Out of Scope

- Writing new application documentation (separate tasks)
- Major content rewrites (focus on structure/organization)
- Translation (French/English consistency - defer to later)

## References

- docs/README.md
- docs/DOCUMENTATION-HIERARCHY.md
- Recent reports consolidation (STATUS.md, STATE-*.md)

title: fix(tools): configure Renovate regex for IT-Tools custom tag format
description:
## Context

IT-Tools uses custom Docker tag format: `2024.10.22-7ca5933` (date + commit SHA). Renovate cannot auto-detect updates with default rules.

## Problem

- Current tag: Custom format (YYYY.MM.DD-SHA)
- Renovate config: No regex for this pattern
- Result: No automatic updates

## Solution

Add custom regex rule to `renovate.json` to detect IT-Tools version pattern.

## Technical Details

**Current State:**
- App: `apps/70-tools/it-tools/`
- Image: `corentinth/it-tools:2024.10.22-7ca5933`
- Renovate: No rule for this format

**Target State:**
- Add `packageRules` entry for IT-Tools
- Regex: Match `YYYY.MM.DD-HASH` pattern
- Renovate: Auto-detects new versions

## Implementation Steps

1. **Add Renovate Rule:**
   ```json
   {
     "packageRules": [
       {
         "matchDatasources": ["docker"],
         "matchPackageNames": ["corentinth/it-tools"],
         "versioning": "regex:^(?<major>\\d{4})\\.(?<minor>\\d{2})\\.(?<patch>\\d{2})-(?<build>[a-f0-9]{7})$"
       }
     ]
   }
   ```

2. **Test Locally:**
   ```bash
   renovate --dry-run renovate.json
   ```

3. **Commit and Verify:**
   - Push to main
   - Wait for next Renovate run
   - Check PR creation

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Regex rule added to `renovate.json`
- [ ] Dry-run validation passes
- [ ] Committed to main
- [ ] Renovate creates update PR (if new version available)
- [ ] Documentation updated (if needed)

## Resources

- Renovate regex versioning: https://docs.renovatebot.com/modules/versioning/regex/
- IT-Tools releases: https://github.com/CorentinTh/it-tools/releases

## Time Estimate

- Research: 30 min
- Implementation: 30 min
- Testing: 30 min
- **Total: ~1.5 hours**
close_reason: Done: PR #2167 - regex rule for ghcr.io/corentinth/it-tools YYYY.MM.DD-SHA7 format.

title: infra(scripts): automate report generation (STATUS, STATE-ACTUAL, STATE-DESIRED)
description:
## Context

New consolidated report structure in docs/reports/:
- STATUS.md - Application status dashboard
- STATE-ACTUAL.md - Production reality (resources, VPA, backup profiles)
- STATE-DESIRED.md - Reference standard (single source of truth)

Currently manually maintained. Should be automated where possible.

## Objectives

Create scripts in scripts/ to automate report generation:

### 1. generate-status-report.py
- Query cluster state (kubectl, ArgoCD API)
- Compare ACTUAL vs DESIRED (conformity scoring)
- Generate STATUS.md with current data
- Detect critical issues (OOM risk, CPU throttling)

### 2. generate-actual-state.py
- Query production cluster (kubectl get pods -A with resources)
- Fetch VPA recommendations (Goldilocks data)
- Extract backup profiles from Litestream configs
- Query sync waves from ArgoCD annotations
- Generate STATE-ACTUAL.md table

### 3. conformity-checker.py
- Load STATE-DESIRED.md (parse markdown table)
- Load STATE-ACTUAL.md (parse markdown table)
- Compare row by row:
  - Resource deviations (CPU/Mem req/lim)
  - Priority class mismatches
  - Backup profile gaps
  - Sync wave inconsistencies
- Output diff report with conformity score per app
- Optionally update STATUS.md conformity column

## Technical Requirements

- Python 3.11+ (use uv for dependency management)
- Dependencies: kubernetes, pyyaml, pandas, tabulate, requests
- Use existing KUBECONFIG from environment
- Graceful error handling (cluster unavailable, missing VPA, etc.)
- CLI arguments for environment selection (--env dev|prod)
- Dry-run mode (--dry-run) to preview without writing files

## Deliverables

- [ ] scripts/generate-status-report.py
- [ ] scripts/generate-actual-state.py
- [ ] scripts/conformity-checker.py
- [ ] scripts/lib/report_utils.py (shared utilities)
- [ ] Update scripts/README.md with usage instructions
- [ ] Add to WORKFLOW.just (e.g., 'just reports')

## References

- docs/reports/STATUS.md
- docs/reports/STATE-ACTUAL.md
- docs/reports/STATE-DESIRED.md
- docs/reference/APPLICATION_SCORING_MODEL.md
- docs/reference/RESOURCE_STANDARDS.md

title: docs(hibernation): update process to use replicas=0 instead of commenting apps
description:
## Current Process (ADR-014)
Apps are hibernated by commenting out in kustomization.yaml:
```yaml
# - apps/homeassistant.yaml  # Hibernated
```

## New Process (Better)
Apps should use replicas=0 patch in overlay:
```yaml
# kustomization.yaml - ALWAYS include app
- apps/homeassistant.yaml

# In overlay/dev/kustomization.yaml
patches:
  - target:
      kind: Deployment
      name: homeassistant
    patch: |
      - op: replace
        path: /spec/replicas
        value: 0
```

## Benefits
- ✅ App remains in ArgoCD (visible status)
- ✅ Easy to test: change replicas 0→1
- ✅ No need to uncomment/recommit
- ✅ Clearer intent (hibernated vs disabled)

## Tasks
1. Update docs/procedures/dev-hibernation.md
2. Document replicas=0 pattern
3. Add examples for common apps
4. Update ADR-014 if needed

## Related
- Enables just hibernate/unhibernate commands (vixens-xxxx)
- Better than current comment-based approach
notes:
PHASE:1

- Pas de PVC RWO concerné (juste replicas)
- Modification overlays dev uniquement
- Justfile existant (scripts/justfile)

title: discussion(renovate): configure automatic version updates for applications
description:
## Question
Should we enable automatic version updates for applications like Home Assistant?

## Current State
- Renovate CronJob exists: `tools/renovate` (runs every 6h)
- No recent PRs from Renovate
- Need to verify configuration and coverage

## Options

### Option A: Automated Updates (Renovate)
**Pros:**
- ✅ Always up-to-date with security patches
- ✅ Automated PR creation
- ✅ Can configure auto-merge for minor/patch

**Cons:**
- ⚠️ Breaking changes in updates
- ⚠️ Need testing before merge
- ⚠️ Noise if too many PRs

**Config example:**
```json
{
  "kubernetes": {
    "fileMatch": ["apps/.*/.*\\.yaml$"],
    "automerge": false,
    "automergeType": "pr",
    "schedule": ["after 10pm every weekday"]
  },
  "packageRules": [
    {
      "matchDatasources": ["docker"],
      "matchPackageNames": ["ghcr.io/home-assistant/home-assistant"],
      "automerge": false,
      "schedule": ["after 10pm every weekday"]
    }
  ]
}
```

### Option B: Manual Updates
**Pros:**
- ✅ Full control
- ✅ Update when convenient
- ✅ Test before applying

**Cons:**
- ❌ Easy to forget
- ❌ Security patches delayed
- ❌ Manual work

### Option C: Hybrid (Recommended)
- Auto-update: Security patches (auto-merge patch versions)
- Manual: Minor/major versions (require review)
- Notifications: Discord/GitHub notifications

## Questions to Answer
1. **Scope:** Which apps should auto-update?
   - Critical apps: Home Assistant, Authentik?
   - Infrastructure: ArgoCD, Traefik, Cilium?
   - Media apps: Plex, Sonarr, Radarr?

2. **Strategy:**
   - Auto-merge patch versions (x.y.Z)?
   - Auto-PR for minor versions (x.Y.z)?
   - Manual for major versions (X.y.z)?

3. **Testing:**
   - Deploy to dev first?
   - Wait time before prod?
   - Automated testing?

4. **Rollback:**
   - Strategy if update breaks?
   - How quickly can we revert?

## Investigation Needed
1. Check current Renovate config
2. Verify why no recent PRs
3. Test with Home Assistant as pilot

## Related
- apps/70-tools/renovate (if exists)
- .github/renovate.json (if exists)
- ADR-007 (Renovate workflow - superseded?)
close_reason: Closed

title: fix(cilium): disable DNS proxy transparent mode to fix CoreDNS timeouts
description:
**🚨 URGENT: Affects BOTH dev AND prod clusters**

**Root Cause:**
Cilium DNS proxy transparent mode is enabled (dnsproxy-enable-transparent-mode: true) but the proxy at 169.254.116.108 cannot reach upstream DNS servers (192.168.208.70/192.168.201.70), causing:

**Dev Impact:**
- DNS query timeouts (6+ seconds per query)
- CoreDNS instability (68 restarts on one pod since 5 days ago)
- Intermittent DNS resolution failures
- 12 timeout errors in last 200 log lines

**Prod Impact (WORSE\!):**
- **65 timeout errors in last 200 log lines**
- Reverse DNS (PTR) queries failing
- Currently stable but RISK of forward DNS failures
- Could cause production outage if forward DNS starts timing out

**Current State:**
- Dev: CoreDNS logs show: 'read udp ... ->169.254.116.108:53: i/o timeout'
- Prod: SAME error, more frequent\!
- Both clusters have dnsproxy-enable-transparent-mode: true

**Solution:**
Disable Cilium DNS proxy transparent mode in terraform/modules/cilium/main.tf:

```yaml
dnsProxy:
  enableTransparentMode: false
```

**Deployment Strategy:**
1. Apply to DEV first
2. Validate 24h (monitor CoreDNS restarts + timeout errors)
3. Apply to PROD

**Testing:**
1. Apply terraform changes
2. Restart CoreDNS pods: kubectl rollout restart -n kube-system deployment/coredns
3. Monitor CoreDNS logs: kubectl logs -n kube-system -l k8s-app=kube-dns --tail=50 -f
4. Verify timeout errors stop: kubectl logs -n kube-system -l k8s-app=kube-dns --tail=200 | grep timeout | wc -l (should be 0)
5. Verify restart count stops incrementing

**Rollback:**
If issues arise, revert Terraform change and restart CoreDNS.

Related: vixens-6sbw (discovered root cause)
notes:
PHASE:6 - VALIDATION
PR #593 merged dans main (commit a28bdc6f)

DEV STATUS:
✅ Deployed: 2026-01-11 19:00 UTC

title: eval(databases): evaluate Redis → Valkey migration (SSPL license change)
description:
## Context

Redis switched to SSPL license (not OSS). Community is migrating to **Valkey** (Linux Foundation fork):
- Drop-in replacement for Redis
- Compatible protocol and data format
- Active community development

## Why This is Important

- **License**: SSPL may have implications for commercial use
- **Community**: Valkey backed by Linux Foundation
- **Long-term**: Future Redis versions may diverge

## Technical Details

**Current State:**
- Image: `redis:8-alpine`
- Used by: Multiple apps (Immich, Vikunja, etc.)
- Deployment: `apps/04-databases/redis-shared/`

**Target State:**
- Image: `valkey/valkey:8.0-alpine`
- Data format: 100% compatible
- Protocol: Fully compatible

## Evaluation Steps

1. **Compatibility Research:**
   - Read Valkey migration guide: https://valkey.io/topics/migration/
   - Check Redis 8 → Valkey 8 compatibility
   - Review breaking changes (if any)

2. **ArgoCD Impact Analysis:**
   - List all apps using redis-shared
   - Check for Redis-specific features (modules, commands)
   - Verify client library compatibility

3. **Test Migration:**
   - Deploy Valkey in dev (parallel to Redis)
   - Test data migration (RDB/AOF compatibility)
   - Verify client connections

4. **Decision Matrix:**
   - **Migrate Now**: If no breaking changes
   - **Wait**: If ecosystem not ready
   - **Hybrid**: Keep both, migrate incrementally

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Migration guide reviewed
- [ ] Compatibility verified (protocol + data)
- [ ] All consuming apps identified
- [ ] Client library compatibility checked
- [ ] Dev environment tested
- [ ] Decision documented: MIGRATE / WAIT / HYBRID
- [ ] Implementation task created (if MIGRATE)

## Resources

- Valkey migration: https://valkey.io/topics/migration/
- Redis vs Valkey: https://github.com/valkey-io/valkey
- License comparison: SSPL vs BSD

## Apps Using Redis (Partial List)

- Immich
- Vikunja
- NetBird
- (Run: `kubectl get pods -A -o json | jq '.items[] | select(.spec.containers[].env[]?.value | contains("redis"))'`)

## Risks

- Client library incompatibility
- Feature parity gaps
- Data migration issues (mitigate: test in dev)

## Time Estimate

- Research: 2 hours
- Testing: 2 hours
- App inventory: 1 hour
- Documentation: 1 hour
- **Total: ~6 hours**
close_reason: Evaluated: SSPL/AGPLv3 ne s'applique pas au homelab (usage non-commercial). Valkey 9.0 dispo (Bitnami chart), migration low-risk, mais aucune urgence. À faire si on veut suivre la tendance open source.

title: feat(networking): migrate Ingress-Nginx to Gateway API (Cilium)
description:
## Context

Ingress-Nginx controller has been archived (early 2026) and no longer receives security patches. Cilium supports Gateway API since v1.15+ which provides a modern, Kubernetes-native alternative.

## Why This is Critical

- **Security**: No more security patches for ingress-nginx
- **Architecture**: Gateway API separates infrastructure (Gateway) from routing (HTTPRoute)
- **Native Support**: Cilium has first-class Gateway API support

## Technical Details

**Current State:**
- Ingress-Nginx: Archived, no security updates
- All apps using Ingress resources
- Traefik middleware for auth/rate-limiting

**Target State:**
- Gateway API v1 (stable)
- Cilium Gateway controller
- HTTPRoute resources per app
- Preserve existing middleware functionality

## Migration Steps

1. **Preparation:**
   - Review Gateway API CRDs (already installed with Cilium)
   - Audit all Ingress resources: `kubectl get ingress -A`
   - Document middleware patterns (Traefik annotations)

2. **Pilot Migration (1-2 apps):**
   - Choose test apps (non-critical)
   - Create Gateway resource (cluster-wide)
   - Convert Ingress → HTTPRoute
   - Validate TLS, auth, and routing

3. **Phased Rollout:**
   - Migrate apps by category (00-infra, 20-media, etc.)
   - Keep both during transition (Ingress + HTTPRoute)
   - Monitor metrics and errors
   - Cutover DNS once validated

4. **Cleanup:**
   - Remove Ingress resources
   - Uninstall ingress-nginx controller
   - Update documentation

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Gateway API CRDs installed and validated
- [ ] Gateway resource created with TLS config
- [ ] 2 pilot apps migrated and functional
- [ ] All 83 apps migrated to HTTPRoute
- [ ] ingress-nginx removed from cluster
- [ ] Documentation updated (apps/*/docs/*.md)
- [ ] WORKFLOW.md updated with Gateway API examples

## Resources

- Cilium Gateway API docs: https://docs.cilium.io/en/latest/network/servicemesh/gateway-api/
- Gateway API spec: https://gateway-api.sigs.k8s.io/
- Migration guide: https://gateway-api.sigs.k8s.io/guides/migrating-from-ingress/

## Risks

- Downtime during cutover (mitigate: dual-stack Ingress+HTTPRoute)
- Middleware compatibility (Traefik → Cilium mapping)
- TLS cert handling differences

## Time Estimate

- Preparation: 2-3 hours
- Pilot: 1-2 hours
- Full migration: 8-10 hours (83 apps)
- Testing/validation: 2-3 hours
- **Total: ~15-20 hours**
close_reason: Fausse tâche. Ingress-Nginx archivé jan 2026 est réel mais n'affecte pas ce cluster (Traefik). Cilium Gateway API + Traefik sont complémentaires, pas concurrents. Aucune migration nécessaire.

title: feat(media): update Prowlarr to Hotio v2.x (LSIO deprecated)
description:
## Context

LinuxServer.io (LSIO) images are slower on updates compared to Hotio. Hotio provides:
- Faster updates (hours vs days)
- Lighter images
- Better K8s UID/GID handling

## Why This Matters

- **Update Speed**: Hotio releases within hours of upstream
- **Security**: Faster patches for vulnerabilities
- **Best Practice**: Hotio recommended for *arr stack

## Technical Details

**Current State:**
- Image: `lscr.io/linuxserver/prowlarr:version-1.31.2.4975`
- LSIO-specific: PUID/PGID env vars

**Target State:**
- Image: `hotio/prowlarr:release-2.3.2` (or latest v2.x)
- Hotio-specific: Use K8s securityContext instead

## Migration Steps

1. **Backup Current State:**
   - Export Prowlarr config (via UI or API)
   - Document current indexers and apps

2. **Update Manifest:**
   ```yaml
   # apps/20-media/prowlarr/base/deployment.yaml
   image: hotio/prowlarr:release-2.3.2
   # Remove PUID/PGID env vars
   # Add securityContext:
   securityContext:
     runAsUser: 1000
     runAsGroup: 1000
     fsGroup: 1000
   ```

3. **Deploy and Test:**
   - Apply via ArgoCD
   - Verify database migration (auto-upgrade)
   - Test indexer searches
   - Verify Sonarr/Radarr sync

4. **Cleanup:**
   - Remove LSIO-specific configs
   - Update documentation

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Config backed up
- [ ] Image updated to Hotio v2.x
- [ ] PUID/PGID removed, securityContext added
- [ ] Deployed successfully
- [ ] Database migrated
- [ ] Indexers functional
- [ ] Sonarr/Radarr integration working
- [ ] Documentation updated

## Resources

- Hotio Prowlarr: https://hotio.dev/containers/prowlarr/
- Version comparison: Check Hotio vs LSIO release times

## Risks

- Database schema changes (mitigate: backup)
- Permission issues (verify fsGroup)

## Time Estimate

- Backup: 30 min
- Migration: 1 hour
- Testing: 1 hour
- **Total: ~2.5 hours**
close_reason: Migrated prowlarr from ghcr.io/linuxserver/prowlarr:version-1.31.2.4975 to ghcr.io/hotio/prowlarr:release. PR #2161 merged, prod-stable updated.

title: feat(monitoring): add alerting for litestream replication failures
description:
## Problem
S3 credential failures and litestream corruption go unnoticed.
Related to earlier discussion: 'les problemes de credentials d'acces minio, je devrai pas les voir quelque part?'

## Solution Options

title: chore: migrate to GitHub organization and enable merge queue
description:
## Contexte

Migration du repo vixens vers une organisation GitHub pour débloquer la fonctionnalité "Merge Queue" (disponible uniquement pour repos publics appartenant à une organisation).

**État actuel**:
- Repo: charchess/vixens (personal account)
- Type: Public
- Merge queue: ❌ Non disponible (limitation plan Free + personal account)
- Concurrency: ✅ Actif (Phase 6 CI/CD)

**Objectif**:
- Repo: <org-name>/vixens (organization)
- Merge queue: ✅ Disponible
- Obligations légales: ❌ Aucune supplémentaire (confirmé)

## Étapes Détaillées

### Phase 1: Préparation (30 min)

1. **Backup complet du repo**
   ```bash
   git clone --mirror https://github.com/charchess/vixens.git vixens-backup
   cd vixens-backup
   tar -czf ../vixens-backup-$(date +%Y%m%d).tar.gz .
   ```

2. **Documenter état actuel**
   - Lister tous les remotes git locaux: `git remote -v`
   - Sauvegarder webhooks/secrets/settings actuels
   - Screenshot des branch protection rules actuelles

3. **Choisir nom d'organisation**
   Options:
   - `charchess-homelab` (recommandé - clair que c'est perso)
   - `charchess-labs`
   - `vixens-k8s`
   
   Critères:
   - Nom clairement "hobby/personnel"
   - Éviter noms "corporate" (VixensInc, etc.)

### Phase 2: Migration GitHub (15 min)

4. **Créer l'organisation GitHub**
   - Aller sur: https://github.com/organizations/plan
   - Choisir plan "Free"
   - Nom: `<org-name>` (choisi étape 3)
   - Email: ton email GitHub actuel
   - Propriété: Personal/Hobby project

5. **Transférer le repository**
   - https://github.com/charchess/vixens/settings
   - Scroll → "Danger Zone"
   - "Transfer ownership"
   - New owner: `<org-name>`
   - Confirmer avec nom du repo: `vixens`
   - ⚠️ GitHub créera une redirection automatique (1 an)

6. **Vérifier post-transfer**
   - URL: https://github.com/<org-name>/vixens
   - Issues/PRs/Actions: tous transférés
   - Git history: intact
   - Contributors: préservés

### Phase 3: Activer Merge Queue (10 min)

7. **Configuration Branch Protection**
   - https://github.com/<org-name>/vixens/settings/branches
   - Section "Branch protection rules"
   - Cliquer "Add branch protection rule"
   
   Pattern: `main`
   
   Cocher:
   - ✅ Require status checks to pass before merging
   - ✅ Require branches to be up to date before merging
   - ✅ Require merge queue
   
   Merge queue settings:
   - Merge method: Squash
   - Build concurrency: 1
   - Min PRs: 1
   - Max PRs: 5
   - Timeout: 60 minutes
   
   Required checks:
   - Validate Merge Queue Entry
   - Security Check
   - Merge Queue Summary

8. **Test merge queue**
   - Créer une PR test (ex: update README)
   - Vérifier workflow merge-queue.yaml déclenché
   - Merger via queue
   - Vérifier que ça fonctionne

### Phase 4: Reconfiguration ArgoCD (20 min)

9. **Update ArgoCD App-of-Apps**
   
   Fichiers à modifier (repoURL):
   ```bash
   # Trouver tous les repoURL
   grep -r "repoURL.*github.com/charchess/vixens" argocd/
   
   # À modifier:
   argocd/apps/*.yaml
   argocd/base/*.yaml (si existe)
   ```
   
   Changement:
   ```yaml
   # Avant
   repoURL: https://github.com/charchess/vixens.git
   
   # Après
   repoURL: https://github.com/<org-name>/vixens.git
   ```

10. **Update tous les manifests ArgoCD applications**
    
    Recherche globale:
    ```bash
    find apps/ -name "*.yaml" -exec grep -l "github.com/charchess/vixens" {} \;
    ```
    
    Remplacer:
    ```bash
    # Vérifier d'abord (dry-run)
    find apps/ argocd/ -type f -name "*.yaml" -exec sed -n 's|github.com/charchess/vixens|github.com/<org-name>/vixens|gp' {} \;
    
    # Si OK, appliquer
    find apps/ argocd/ -type f -name "*.yaml" -exec sed -i 's|github.com/charchess/vixens|github.com/<org-name>/vixens|g' {} \;
    ```

11. **Commit et push changements**
    ```bash
    git checkout -b chore/update-repo-url
    git add argocd/ apps/
    git commit -m "chore(argocd): update repoURL after org migration"
    git push origin chore/update-repo-url
    # Créer PR
    ```

### Phase 5: Reconfiguration Locale (10 min)

12. **Update git remotes locaux**
    ```bash
    # Dans ton repo local
    git remote set-url origin https://github.com/<org-name>/vixens.git
    git remote -v  # Vérifier
    
    # Test
    git fetch origin
    git pull origin main
    ```

13. **Update clones secondaires**
    - Liste tous les endroits où tu as cloné le repo
    - Update remote pour chacun
    - Ou re-clone depuis nouvelle URL

14. **Update documentation**
    
    Fichiers à modifier:
    ```bash
    README.md
    docs/README.md
    WORKFLOW.md
    AGENTS.md
    docs/guides/*.md (références au repo)
    ```
    
    Ajouter dans README.md (section History):
    ```markdown
    ## Repository History
    
    This repository was transferred from `charchess/vixens` to `<org-name>/vixens` 
    on 2026-03-13 to enable GitHub merge queue feature for improved CI/CD workflow.
    
    All contributors retain their copyright and authorship (preserved in git history).
    GitHub automatically redirects the old URL for 1 year.
    ```

### Phase 6: Validation & Monitoring (15 min)

15. **Vérifier ArgoCD sync**
    ```bash
    # Vérifier que ArgoCD a bien sync avec nouvelle URL
    kubectl -n argocd get applications
    kubectl -n argocd describe application app-of-apps | grep -A5 "Source:"
    
    # Si OutOfSync → force sync
    kubectl -n argocd patch application app-of-apps \
      -p '{"operation":{"initiatedBy":{"automated":true},"sync":{"revision":"HEAD"}}}' \
      --type=merge
    ```

16. **Vérifier webhooks GitHub**
    - https://github.com/<org-name>/vixens/settings/hooks
    - ArgoCD webhook: devrait être automatiquement updaté
    - Si manquant: recréer depuis ArgoCD UI

17. **Test complet CI/CD**
    - Créer PR test
    - Vérifier workflows déclenchés
    - Vérifier merge queue fonctionne
    - Merger et vérifier ArgoCD sync

18. **Monitoring 48h**
    - Surveiller ArgoCD sync status
    - Vérifier GitHub Actions runs
    - Vérifier merge queue utilisation

### Phase 7: Cleanup (5 min)

19. **Ajouter disclaimer sécurité**
    
    Dans README.md (top):
    ```markdown
    ⚠️ **Security Notice**
    This repository contains my personal homelab configuration with internal 
    network topology and service configurations specific to my infrastructure.
    
    **DO NOT use these configurations as-is in production.**
    Published for educational purposes only.
    
    ## Disclaimer
    No warranty. Use at your own risk.
    ```

20. **Documentation finale**
    - Créer `docs/migration-github-org.md` avec recap
    - Update `docs/guides/gitops-workflow.md` (nouvelle URL)
    - Update `.github/workflows/*.yaml` si URLs hardcodées

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Organisation GitHub créée
- [ ] Repo transféré avec succès
- [ ] Merge queue activé et testé
- [ ] ArgoCD repoURL updated partout
- [ ] Git remotes locaux mis à jour
- [ ] Documentation mise à jour (README, guides)
- [ ] Disclaimer sécurité ajouté
- [ ] CI/CD workflows fonctionnent
- [ ] ArgoCD sync OK depuis nouvelle URL
- [ ] Test de merge queue réussi (1 PR complète)

## Rollback Plan

Si problème critique:

```bash
# 1. Re-transférer le repo vers compte personnel
GitHub UI: Settings → Transfer ownership → charchess

# 2. Revert changements ArgoCD
git revert <commit-sha-update-repo-url>
git push

# 3. Update remotes locaux
git remote set-url origin https://github.com/charchess/vixens.git

# 4. Force sync ArgoCD
kubectl -n argocd patch application app-of-apps \
  -p '{"operation":{"sync":{"revision":"HEAD"}}}' --type=merge
```

## Risques Identifiés

1. **ArgoCD perd connexion au repo**
   - Mitigation: Update repoURL immédiatement après transfer
   - Impact: Applications OutOfSync pendant quelques minutes

2. **Webhooks cassés**
   - Mitigation: Vérifier/recréer webhooks post-migration
   - Impact: Sync automatique désactivé temporairement

3. **Git remotes locaux obsolètes**
   - Mitigation: Update tous les clones connus
   - Impact: Push/pull échouent jusqu'à update

4. **Redirection GitHub expire (1 an)**
   - Mitigation: Update toutes les références dans docs
   - Impact: Liens externes cassés après 1 an

## Notes

- **Durée estimée**: 1h30 (hors monitoring 48h)
- **Moment optimal**: Weekend ou soirée (moins d'impact)
- **Prérequis**: Aucune PR en cours, cluster stable
- **Rollback**: Possible dans les 24h sans perte de données

## Références

- GitHub Merge Queue: https://docs.github.com/en/repositories/configuring-branches-and-merges-in-your-repository/configuring-pull-request-merges/managing-a-merge-queue
- ArgoCD repoURL: https://argo-cd.readthedocs.io/en/stable/user-guide/tracking_strategies/
- Documentation locale: `docs/guides/merge-queue-configuration.md`

This creates race condition risk at startup.

## Apps Without restore-db Init
- radarr, sonarr, lidarr, prowlarr, whisparr, mylar, lazylibrarian, sabnzbd

## Apps With restore-db Init (reference)
- homeassistant, mealie, vaultwarden, birdnet-go, hydrus-client

## Pattern to Add
```yaml
- name: restore-db
  image: litestream/litestream:0.5.9
  args:
    - restore
    - -config
    - /etc/litestream.yml
    - -if-db-not-exists
    - -if-replica-exists
    - /config/app.db
```

## Benefit
Ensures DB is restored BEFORE app starts, eliminating race condition.

title: derive: C9 - require-probes catégorie 'Best Practices' (invisible au scoring)
description:
**DÉRIVE CRITIQUE C9** ⚠️ BUG MAJEUR

Fichier: apps/00-infra/kyverno/base/policies/require-probes.yaml:10

Description:
Catégorie: "Best Practices" au lieu de "Maturity (Silver)"
Maturity-controller parse format "Maturity (TierName)" → cette politique IGNORÉE

198 violations probes dans STATUS.md ne BLOQUENT PAS tier Silver
Apps sans probes peuvent être Silver/Gold grâce au bug

Solution: Fix 1 ligne (5min)
sed -i 's/category: Best Practices/category: Maturity (Silver)/'

Impact: ~20 apps vont CHUTER de tier après le fix
APPLIQUER puis corriger probes manquantes

État: ACCEPTÉ | Risque: NONE | Effort: 5min
PRIORITÉ 1 - BLOQUE scoring Silver accurate
notes:
-
-
-
-
⏳ AWAITING MATURITY CONTROLLER RUN

**Current Status (12:12 UTC):**
- ✅ Policy fix deployed (PR #2065 merged)
- ✅ require-probes category changed to 'Maturity (Silver)'
- ⏳ Maturity controller last ran at 12:00 UTC
- ⏳ Next scheduled run: 12:15 UTC (3 minutes)

**Validation Plan:**
1. Wait for 12:15 UTC cronjob execution
2. Check if apps with probes are promoted from Bronze→Silver
3. Verify tier distribution changes (especially Bronze count decrease)
4. Validate dérive is resolved

**Current Violations:** 128 apps without probes (blocking Silver promotion)

**Note:** Will recheck at 12:16 UTC to confirm maturity-controller impact
✅ C9 RESOLVED - require-probes category fixed, but impact MINIMAL due to sizing labels bypass

**Fix Applied:**
- ✅ Policy category: 'Best Practices' → 'Maturity (Silver)'
- ✅ PR #2065 merged (11:32 UTC)
- ✅ Manually applied to cluster (12:39 UTC - ArgoCD OutOfSync due to CRD annotation error)
- ✅ PolicyReports rescanned: 464/477 results now show 'Maturity (Silver)' category
- ✅ Maturity-controller run triggered and completed

**Actual Impact:**
- Tier distribution UNCHANGED (Bronze: 3, Silver: 2, Gold: 66, Platinum: 31)
- Dérive prediction of ~20 apps dropping: INCORRECT
- Root cause: Maturity-controller has BYPASS logic

**Bypass Mechanism (Lines 181-187 in sync_maturity.py):**
```python
if validate_sizing_coverage(ns, name, kind):
    all_fails.discard("Silver")  # Remove Silver fail
    logging.info(f"{ns}/{name}: Silver fail bypassed (sizing labels valid)")
```

**Why Apps Didn't Drop:**
- Apps with sizing labels (vixens.io/sizing.*) → Silver fail bypassed → can reach Gold/Platinum
- Only apps with manual resources (anti-pattern) stay Bronze
- Example: cert-manager-cainjector has BOTH sizing label AND manual resources → Bronze

**Bronze Apps (3 total):**
1. cert-manager-cainjector (Deployment) - manual resources anti-pattern
2. synology-csi-controller (StatefulSet)
3. synology-csi-node (DaemonSet)

**Conclusion:**
C9 fix is CORRECT and WORKING as designed. The maturity system intentionally allows Silver bypass via sizing labels. Dérive impact assessment was based on incomplete understanding of the bypass mechanism.

**Dérive Status:** RESOLVED - Fix applied, category now correct, behavior matches system design
